issues_found: Source is approximately 8 months old (July 2025 vs. March 2026 assessment date) - acceptable for archival value but reduces recency score
Source body was truncated at end; key monetary policy content appears complete but cannot verify if any content was omitted
human_review_reason: Record is well-constructed and factually supported by source. Minor concerns are age (within acceptable range for historical documents) and truncated source body, but neither warrants human review as the core content is verifiable and accurate.

corrected_fields.market_structure_context: The ECB's APP and PEPP portfolios are declining at a measured pace as the Eurosystem no longer reinvests maturing securities principal. Credit standards for business loans were broadly unchanged in Q2 2025 per the July 2025 bank lending survey, with banks' concerns about economic risks offset by competition among lenders. Corporate bond issuance grew at 3.4% annual rate while loan growth moderated to 2.5%, suggesting firms are partially shifting to market-based financing. Mortgage lending growth edged up to 2.0% amid stronger demand despite slightly tightened credit standards.
